title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies · Git Collaboration Room
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first merge topology and strategy decision record for merge bases, topology, and integration strategies. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of merge topology and strategy decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.
Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G13 is a workshop in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Gita Merge Result Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Gita Merge Result Defense record says merge topology and strategy decision record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve audit the proposed evidence packet.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-1q2gco" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Merge Result Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-1q2gco
# scenario-key: trace-1q2gco probe-ocg2q1 boundary-2gco1q owner-1q2gcox fault-1q2gcoz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1q candidate=b00b1co
# hypothesis: criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor
git merge-base --all release topic
# expected: all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed
# observation: two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration
# changed-case: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base
# after-ref: main=a11ce1q candidate=c0ffeeo
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy
# transfer-limit: validate resulting behavior in an authorized disposable clone because graph evidence is not semantic proof
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Merge Result Defense structural check is only one gate. merge topology and strategy decision record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Gita Merge Result Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G22 is a workshop in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy. Competency probe: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Merge Result Defense through the concrete merge topology and strategy decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.

hints: Name the Gita Merge Result Defense precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G22 is a workshop in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy. Competency probe: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A conflict-free merge proves both sides remain behaviorally compatible.
